channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: PirloTV sports piracy network disrupted as 44 domains seized
item.description: A major sports piracy ring linked to the illegal PirloTV streaming platform has been disrupted in an action that targeted 44 domains. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Bluekit phishing kit adopts browser-in-the-middle for login theft
item.description: The Bluekit phishing-as-a-service platform continues to evolve with nearly 70 new hostnames identified over the past week and by adding browser-in-the-middle capabilities for improved data theft. [......
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chrome Ad Blocker with 10M+ Installs Found with Dormant Script Injection Capability
item.description:
An analysis of a popular Google Chrome ad block extension for YouTube has uncovered the ability to execute arbitrary JavaScript code.
According to Island, the extension, named Adblock for YouTube (ID...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Four Elevations of Effective Fraud Prevention
item.description: Fraudsters don't attack just one transaction. They target accounts, platforms, and entire ecosystems. IPQS explains the four elevations of fraud prevention and why broader visibility improves fraud de...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Smart TV Proxyware, 24-Year curl Bug, AI Crime Forums + 13 More Stories
item.description:
It’s dumb out there again.
This week has the usual smell of prod on fire and nobody wanting to admit who left the door open — old creds still working, trusted apps doing sketchy crap, browser tricks ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Webinar: Why account takeovers remain one of the hardest threats to stop
item.description: Account takeover attacks continue to challenge security teams because attackers often operate through legitimate accounts and trusted services. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help organiz...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Surviving the Mythos Era: Richard Bejtlich on the Case for NDR
item.description: Despite the abundance of telemetry at analysts’ disposal, many security operations teams struggle to answer a few basic questions during incident investigation: What happened? What evidence do we have...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Europe Evolves Into Ransomware's Favorite Region
item.description: After a global lull, ransomware gangs are setting sights on a rich new arena: attacking EU organizations and their suppliers....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Gaslight macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Disrupt AI-Assisted Analysis
item.description: A previously undocumented Rust-based macOS implant and information stealer has been found to embed a prompt injection payload designed to trick a malware analyst's artificial intelligence (AI) tools a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Mistic Backdoor Linked to KongTuke in ClickFix and ModeloRAT Campaigns
item.description: A new, stealthy backdoor named Mistic has been deployed as part of suspected financially motivated attacks aimed at multiple organizations spanning insurance, education, IT, and professional services ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Zero-Day CVE-2026-20245 Exploited to Gain Root Access
item.description: An unknown threat actor exploited a recently disclosed high-severity security flaw impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN as a zero-day at least two months before it was publicly disclosed, according to new ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google releases new privacy controls for activity history, personalization
item.description: Google is rolling out new privacy controls for Search services and Google Play, giving you more control over saved history and personalized recommendations. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DraftKings hacker 'Snoopy' sentenced to 18 months in prison
item.description: A 21-year-old using the alias "Snoopy" was sentenced to 18 months in prison for his role in hacking DraftKings accounts in the November 2022 cyberattack. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mandiant reveals how Cisco SD-WAN zero-day attacks gained root access
item.description: New details have been revealed on how hackers exploited a Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-20245 in zero-day attacks to create rogue root accounts on targeted devices. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Attackers Hit Cisco SD-WAN Flaw 2 Months Before Disclosure
item.description: Researchers believe rogue peering was used to connect to the victim's SD-WAN devices to gain admin privileges and root-level access....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Malicious Edge extension abuses Native Messaging as bridge to malware
item.description: A malicious Microsoft Edge extension dubbed 'Edgecution' has been used in a ransomware attack to escape the browser sandbox and deploy a Python-based backdoor. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Faces Surge in Cyber Threats
item.description: Persistent cybercrime, social engineering, and infrastructure threats continue to plague the FIFA 2026 World Cup across the US, Canada, and Mexico....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Do CISOs Need a Code of Ethics?
item.description: Dark Reading Confidential Episode 19: Kickbacks, no-show jobs, "dirty" VCs, and shelf ware — industry expert Robert "RSnake" Hansen explains why he thinks its time for a CISO code of ethics to ensure ...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CISA Warns Critical Lantronix EDS5000 Flaw Is Being Actively Exploited
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday warned of active exploitation of a critical security flaw impacting Lantronix EDS5000 Series devices, urging Federal Civilia...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: More Malicious OpenClaw Skills Threaten AI Supply Chain
item.description: OpenClaw removed five packages from its ClawHub skills marketplace that bypassed security checks even though they included infostealers and other threats....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Amadey and StealC Malware Network Disrupted, 27M Stolen Credentials Recovered

item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA warns of max severity Ubiquiti flaws exploited in attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning of hackers actively exploiting flaws in Ubiquity UniFi OS and Lantronix serial-to-ethernet servers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Amadey, StealC malware operations disrupted in Operation Endgame action
item.description: Microsoft, Europol, and international partners have disrupted infrastructure used by the Amadey and StealC malware operations as part of Operation Endgame, which targets cybercriminal services and ran...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Securing the service desk: Why social engineering attacks keep succeeding
item.description: Service desks have become a favored target for attackers seeking password resets, MFA changes, and access to corporate accounts. Specops Software breaks down how service desk social engineering attack...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cordyceps CI/CD Flaws Expose 300+ GitHub Repositories to Supply-Chain Attacks
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new class of CI/CD workflow weakness that allows attackers to hijack workflows and compromise open-source supply chains.
The "critical exploitable pattern" ha...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
item.description: Attackers can exploit the issue to disable security and integrated browser tools without needing administrator privileges or kernel exploits....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Dawn of the Apex Agentic Adversary
item.description:
We are standing at the end of an era we never thought to mourn: the era of human-speed threats.
For years, cybersecurity moved to a rhythm organizations could follow. A researcher found a bug, a CVE ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Stealthy Mistic backdoor linked to ransomware access broker KongTuke
item.description: A new backdoor dubbed Mistic has been observed in financially motivated attacks targeting organizations in the insurance, education, IT, and professional services sectors. [...]...

item.title: DoJ Seizes Huione Cloud Account Tied to Cyber Scam Money Laundering

item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco Unified CM Flaw Exploited After PoC Reveals File-Write Path to Root
item.description: Threat actors have begun to exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) and Unified Communications Manager Session Management Editio...
